description:
A former engineer left an un-rotated set of application credentials in
    /home/user/credentials.  
    Your job, as the new security engineer, is to perform a clean
    “credential-rotation” cycle entirely from the Linux terminal.

    Initial system state that you can rely on  
      • Directory: /home/user/credentials  
      • File:      /home/user/credentials/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt  
        └── The file currently exists and is readable by you.  
            Its exact content does **not** matter for this task, you simply
            have to preserve it as an historical record.

    Target end state that the automated test suite will verify
    (follow the steps in any order, but ALL artefacts below must exist when
    you are done):

    1. Create the directory /home/user/credentials/archive if it does not
       already exist.

    2. Move the file app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt into that archive directory
       and rename it to app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak  
          Final absolute path:
          /home/user/credentials/archive/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak

    3. Inside the *same* archive directory, create a compressed backup of the
       .bak file named  
          /home/user/credentials/archive/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak.tar.gz  
       • The archive must be a valid gzip-compressed tarball that contains
         exactly one file: app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak  
       • After creating the .tar.gz you must leave the original .bak file in
         place (do not delete it).

    4. Generate a *new* credential file in /home/user/credentials called  
          /home/user/credentials/app_secrets_2023-10-01.txt  
       The file must contain **exactly** three newline-separated key=value
       pairs in the order shown below (no extra spaces, no trailing blank
       line):

           CLIENT_ID=2f3b0f2b-4c5d-49c8-92d5-7b8cebb4a123
           CLIENT_SECRET=874d2e31-34ef-4c27-9e1c-abcd9876ef01
           ROTATED_ON=2023-10-01T15:30:00Z

       After the file is created, ensure its permissions are set to 600
       (read/write only for the current user).

    5. Append a single-line entry to /home/user/credentials/rotation_history.log  
       The line must follow **exactly** this format (single line, spaces
       exactly as written, no newline before, *one* newline after):

           2023-10-01T15:30:00Z ROTATED old_file=archive/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak new_file=app_secrets_2023-10-01.txt compressed=archive/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak.tar.gz

       • If rotation_history.log does not yet exist, create it.  
       • DO NOT alter or delete any previous lines that might already be
         present in the file—only append.

    6. Directory listing expectations  
       When you run
           ls -1R /home/user/credentials
       the test harness will verify that the resulting relative structure
       matches **exactly** the following order and names (directories first,
       then files, alphabetically inside each directory):

           /home/user/credentials:
           app_secrets_2023-10-01.txt
           archive
           rotation_history.log

           /home/user/credentials/archive:
           app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak
           app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak.tar.gz

    7. File content & permission expectations  
       The harness will also explicitly check:

       • cat /home/user/credentials/app_secrets_2023-10-01.txt  
         must return exactly the three lines listed in step 4 (no extra
         whitespace).

       • tar -tzf /home/user/credentials/archive/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak.tar.gz  
         must output a single entry:  
             app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak

       • stat -c "%a" /home/user/credentials/app_secrets_2023-10-01.txt  
         must output:  
             600

       • The final line of /home/user/credentials/rotation_history.log must
         match the log-line specified in step 5 verbatim.

    Summary  
      You are done when the directory tree, file contents, permissions and
      log entry satisfy **all** checks above.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth layout and contents that the automated checker will use
        to verify the agent’s work.  DO NOT reveal these values in the public
        <task> element.
    -->
    <dir path="/home/user/credentials">
        <file name="app_secrets_2023-10-01.txt" mode="600"><![CDATA[
CLIENT_ID=2f3b0f2b-4c5d-49c8-92d5-7b8cebb4a123
CLIENT_SECRET=874d2e31-34ef-4c27-9e1c-abcd9876ef01
ROTATED_ON=2023-10-01T15:30:00Z
]]></file>
        <file name="rotation_history.log" append="true"><![CDATA[
2023-10-01T15:30:00Z ROTATED old_file=archive/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak new_file=app_secrets_2023-10-01.txt compressed=archive/app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak.tar.gz
]]></file>
        <dir name="archive">
            <file name="app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak" mode="644" />
            <file name="app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak.tar.gz" mode="644">
                <!-- tarball must contain exactly 'app_secrets_2023-09-15.txt.bak' -->
            </file>
        </dir>
    </dir>
